title: The Power Of Hell
content:
�There is no place,� he said,
    �For love or pity here;
    We dread and only dread
    The moods that once were dear.

    �We break the ancient spell,
    And arm to take our part
    Against the power of Hell.�
    And Hell was in his heart.
meaning:
John Le Gay Brereton's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Power Of Hell"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
